items.title: Teenagers brandish knives, hammer at Canberra shopping centre

children.content: Police say they were called to the Canberra Centre about 6:20pm on Friday after reports that a group of young people were involved in a disturbance, brandishing knives and a hammer.

image.altText: A police car with its lights on outside a shopping centre.

items.title: Driver sentenced to 13 years' jail over hit-and-run of Canberra schoolboys

children.content: It's almost a year to the day since Tayler Hazell drove a stolen car erratically through Canberra, seriously injuring two children.

image.altText: Three police officers stand talking seriously next to a road with cones on it.

items.title: Fuel costs drive COVID-like surge in demand for emergency relief

children.content: Rising petrol and diesel prices are driving Canberra families to seek emergency assistance and forcing small businesses to consider whether to pass on higher costs to customers.

items.title: 'Forget it': NSW beachgoers urged to cancel plans amid hazardous weather

children.content: New South Wales beaches will be too dangerous to visit this weekend, with authorities issuing a warning to rock fishers, swimmers and boaters to stay away from the coastline.

image.altText: A large wave with surfers and swimmers beyond it and houses seen in the distance.

items.title: 'Designed by youth': Service to offer free trauma support for Canberra teens

children.content: The new service, co-designed by young people, will provide therapy, peer support, and family services for teenagers experiencing trauma.

image.altText: A woman with long dark hair leans against a consultation room door smiling.

items.title: 'Electrons to the people:' Neighbours build commercial solar farm

children.content: Hundreds of Goulburn residents combine to build their own commercial solar farm, demonstrating the community benefits of renewable energy.

gallery.altText: Family of six sitting and smailing amongst a solar farm

articles.title: Manly sacks coach Seibold after three games of 2026 season

children.content: Manly has sacked head coach Anthony Seibold after a dismal start to his fourth NRL season at the helm.

image.altText: A coach looks on before a rugby league match

articles.title: Broncos 'surprised' as key cog resigns after dispute with coach

children.content: Brisbane defensive coach Ben Te'o, the mastermind behind the scrambling defence in the 2025 grand final win, hands in his resignation.

image.altText: Ben Te'o gestures while speaking to Michael Maguire at a Broncos training session

children.content: The Broncos overpowered the Dolphins 26-22 in the Battle of Brisbane, after Wests Tigers stunned ladder leaders New Zealand Warriors in Auckland. Follow live.

updates.title: FT: Broncos end tough week on a high
HT: Broncos lead Dolphins in tense Battle of Brisbane

items.title: AFL players to lose insurance coverage for brain injuries

children.content: More than 500 AFL players will soon no longer be covered by insurance for brain injuries in their superannuation after Zurich Insurance moved to cut their ability to claim for concussion and head trauma.

image.altText: A football on the ground during Hawthorn Hawks' training session at Waverley Park in 2008.

items.title: Pendlebury downplays injury as Giant bursts ear drum in loss to Pies

children.content: Collingwood veteran Scott Pendlebury's night comes to a premature end in the third quarter as the Pies benefit from some wasteful kicking from the Giants. Follow live.

image.altText: Oscar Steene celebrates his first goal with his teammates

items.title: The 'crown jewels' of the Iranian nuclear program and why the US wants them

children.content: A move from Israel or the United States to find and secure Iran's enriched uranium stockpile would be dangerous, but experts say it is one of the only ways to end the war and prevent it from returning.

image.altText: A satellite image of various buildings in Iran.

items.title: Liberals to retain SA opposition status despite One Nation's second seat, ABC projects

children.content: The ABC is projecting that the Liberals have retained the Adelaide Hills seat of Heysen, meaning the party will be the second largest in the House of Assembly and retain opposition status.

image.altText: Man in suit speaking to camera.

items.title: Jury clears men accused of raping intoxicated woman waiting for an Uber

children.content: Two men accused of raping a young woman they picked up while she waited for an Uber have been acquitted on all charges by a Northern Territory jury.

image.altText: Close ups of both men

items.title: NT working to repair flood damaged roads, with more than 1,000 potholes filled

children.content: More than 100 sections of road are closed or impassible following widespread flooding across the Northern Territory, with the cost of repairs set to rise with fuel prices.

image.altText: A wet, damaged road has chunks of bitumen missing.

items.title: New Spirit of Tasmania ferry to be used as Dark Mofo floating art gallery

children.content: The new spirit of Tasmania ferries might not be available for journeys across the Bass Strait yet — but this winter, one of them will take on a new role as an art gallery, during Hobart's Dark Mofo festival.

children.content: Fire up your remotes, headphones or over and get set for a weekend of entertainment.

children.content: It's been a big week in the news. Did you catch these stories?

items.title: Inflation set to soar as fuel costs hit prices of everyday items from bread to ridesharing

children.content: Fuel surcharges are being applied across a range of industries, from the delivery of bricks to building sites to Australia's biggest rideshare companies, meaning consumer prices are rising.

image.altText: A man in a grocery store smiles at the camera.

items.title: Katherine's worst flood in 28 years leaves survivors with disaster 'PTSD'

children.content: As it recovers from its worst flooding in decades, those living in the NT town of Katherine are trying to come to terms with the ongoing threat on their doorstep.

image.altText: A man stands next to a fence covered in mud.

items.title: Hatchet attack on dog at SA golf course prompts public appeal

children.content: A kelpie that was found with "horrific" head wounds at an Adelaide Hills golf course has prompted the RSPCA to issue a public appeal for information.
Warning: This story contains details some readers may find distressing.

image.altText: A dog with stitches in its head.

items.title: Justice department confirms not guilty plea by ex-National Trust Tasmania boss on theft charges

children.content: Following the lifting of a suppression order, Tasmania's department of justice has confirmed the former director of the National Trust in Tasmania has pleaded not guilty to stealing almost $150,000 worth of items from his former employer.

image.altText: A sign at the front of a glass and brick building which reads 'Supreme Court'.

items.title: Parts of NSW wake up to 'surreal' snowfall in March

children.content: It is still only March, but snow is falling in parts of NSW and the Bureau of Meteorology is checking its record books.

items.title: 'I've got a family to support': Sandilands's battle to stay on air in court

children.content: Kyle Sandilands says he wants to "get back to work as quick as possible" as a legal battle between the controversial radio host and his former employer reaches court for the first time.

image.altText: Kyle Sandilands has white hair and a beard and is surrounded by reporters.

items.title: Fans rethink driving to SA for AFL Gather Round as global fuel crisis continues

children.content: The SA government and AFL are in discussions to try to keep fans flocking to Adelaide for next month's AFL Gather Round, as the global fuel crisis threatens to disrupt travel plans.

image.altText: Multiple yellow signs reading 'pump not in use' attached to petrol pumps at a station

items.title: Police search remote Far North Queensland property where human remains found

children.content: The remains were found in the yard after police were called to the property for a welfare check on a 70-year-old man. An identity has not yet been confirmed.

image.altText: Two people wearing protective gear, gloves, and masks appearing to inspect a piece of material in a garden.

items.title: Queensland man charged with 3D printing gun parts after police raid

children.content: Police say the charges come amid a concerning rise in the use of 3D printing technology to manufacture weapons.

image.altText: 3D-printed firearm and parts laid on a bed spread.

items.title: Nazeem Hussain developed his 'gut-punch' comedy over a lifetime of racism

children.content: For comedian Nazeem Hussain, the best way to respond to war, discrimination and racism is to laugh about it. And he's built his whole career on this special brand of risky, outrageous comedy.

items.title: Qld opposition says government broke promise on adult crime, adult time advice

children.content: A report from the expert legal panel advising the state government's adult crime, adult time policy has been released, explaining the framework for the advice, rather than everything provided to the government.

image.altText: Laura Gerber speaking at a lectern.

items.title: Diesel fuel stolen after thieves break into rural property

children.content: Several hundred litres of diesel fuel have been stolen during an overnight break-in on a rural property in central Tasmania.

image.altText: Tasmania Police scene tape, generic image.

items.title: Can states cap fuel prices? Tasmanian emergency fuel laws explained

children.content: The Tasmanian government is seeking to have the power to cap petrol prices. But how will it work? And if it does, can other states do the same?

image.altText: Tasmanian petrol prices falling

items.title: Aboriginal woman seeks on-country court hearing for trespass case

children.content: A member of the Tasmanian Aboriginal community charged with trespass has applied to the Hobart Magistrates Court to have her case heard on Aboriginal-owned land.

image.altText: People gathered outside court with an Aboriginal flag

items.title: How fighting racist bikies helped Henry Sue reach kung fu mastery

children.content: Henry Sue says kung fu taught him how to stand up for himself and live an honourable life.
